message:
Running command: 
# Strategy: Re-run DRT again with a CONTINUOUS pipe drainer to prevent the deadlock.
# The run will re-run DRT from scratch (using the same pre-DRT ODB from step 67).
# This time we keep the pipe drained constantly.

MCU_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu"
DESIGN="mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper"

echo "=== Starting DRT re-run WITH pipe drainer at $(date) ==="

# Start LibreLane with --last-run (uses wrapper_run tag, steps from DRT onward)
cd "$MCU_DIR/openlane/${DESIGN}"

# Launch librelane in background with output to file
nohup librelane \
  --pdk-root /nc/apps/pdk \
  --pdk sky130A \
  --last-run \
  -F OpenROAD.DetailedRouting \
  config.json \
  > /tmp/librelane_drt2.log 2>&1 &
LR_PID=$!
echo "LibreLane PID: $LR_PID"

# Wait a moment for librelane to start and spawn OpenROAD
sleep 10
echo "=== Checking initial state ==="
ps aux | grep -E "(openroad|librelane)" | grep -v grep | awk '{print "PID:"$2, "CMD:"$11, "CPU:"$3"%"}'

# Find the LibreLane python process
LIBPID=$(ps aux | grep "librelane.*config.json" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
echo "LibreLane PID: $LIBPID"

message:
Running command: 
LIBPID=1100961
ORPID=1101105

# Start a CONTINUOUS and FAST pipe drainer to prevent the deadlock
# This reads from LibreLane's pipe fd 11 and saves the content
nohup bash -c "
LIBPID=$LIBPID
PIPE_PATH=/proc/\$LIBPID/fd/11
EXTRA_LOG=/tmp/drt2_pipe.log
echo 'Drainer started at \$(date)' > \"\$EXTRA_LOG\"
while [ -e \"\$PIPE_PATH\" ]; do
    dd if=\"\$PIPE_PATH\" bs=4096 count=32 >> \"\$EXTRA_LOG\" 2>/dev/null
    # If dd returns non-zero (pipe closed), exit
    [ \$? -ne 0 ] && echo 'Pipe closed at \$(date)' >> \"\$EXTRA_LOG\" && break
done
echo 'Drainer exited at \$(date)' >> \"\$EXTRA_LOG\"
" > /dev/null 2>&1 &
DRAINER_PID=$!
echo "Pipe drainer PID: $DRAINER_PID"

# Monitor progress
sleep 15
echo "=== Progress check $(date) ==="
tail -20 /tmp/drt2_pipe.log 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
RUN_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"
ls "$RUN_DIR/" | sort -V | tail -5
echo "---"
ps aux | grep openroad | grep -v grep | awk '{print "PID:"$2,"CPU:"$3"%","TIME:"$10}'

# The drainer isn't reading fast enough with the dd approach since dd blocks until `count` is reached.
# Use Python for a proper non-blocking reader

LIBPID=1100961

# Kill old drainers
kill 1101734 1102413 2>/dev/null || true

# Python pipe drainer - reads everything available continuously
nohup python3 -c "
import os, sys, time
PIPE = '/proc/$LIBPID/fd/11'
LOG = '/tmp/drt2_pipe.log'
with open(LOG, 'ab') as out:
    out.write(b'Python drainer started\n')
    out.flush()
    fd = os.open(PIPE, os.O_RDONLY | os.O_NONBLOCK)
    try:
        while True:
            try:
                data = os.read(fd, 65536)
                if data:
                    out.write(data)
                    out.flush()
                else:
                    time.sleep(0.01)
            except BlockingIOError:
                time.sleep(0.05)
            except OSError:
                break
    finally:
        os.close(fd)
        out.write(b'Python drainer exited\n')
        out.flush()
" > /dev/null 2>&1 &
